title: The Bart Wants What It Wants

jokes.text: No! The Olympics have preempted my favorite shows for the last time.
jokes.setup: Homer is being chased by Olympic officials for stealing the torch
jokes.punchline: His motivation is petty TV schedule complaints rather than anything noble
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - taking something grand (Olympic torch) and reducing it to mundane personal grievances. Strong craft for character specificity.

jokes.text: You wait till next month! You wait till next month!
jokes.setup: Marge suggests waiting until next month for his shows
jokes.punchline: Homer's childish repetitive tantrum response
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Homer's immaturity but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Dad, you told me there were no private schools in Springfield.
jokes.setup: Family discovers private school at fair
jokes.punchline: Homer's previous lie to avoid expensive school costs exposed
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff revealing Homer's parental deception for financial reasons. Well-structured joke.

jokes.text: But knowing about it would make you want to go here.
jokes.setup: Lisa catches him in the lie about private schools
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact admission of his deceptive parenting logic
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - completely honest about being dishonest. Perfect character voice and economy.

jokes.text: 10-4, Princess. Is that your beer tent?
jokes.setup: Formal headmaster introduction at elite school
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately asks about alcohol at fundraiser
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer behavior but predictable. Works for character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh! Is that where Boozer drank the pee? / It's one of the places.
jokes.setup: Headmaster mentions film shot on campus
jokes.punchline: Homer focuses on crude detail, headmaster's resigned response
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Homer's lowbrow focus and the headmaster's weary acceptance.

jokes.text: And our school board's cut us back to 16. All of them lanthanides.
jokes.setup: Private school has 250 elements on periodic table
jokes.punchline: Public school cut to only lanthanides - the most obscure elements
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist joke requiring chemistry knowledge. The specificity of 'lanthanides' makes it exceptionally clever.

jokes.text: Here's your bond issue!
jokes.setup: Discussion about funding school equipment through bonds
jokes.punchline: Skinner physically destroys something instead
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy. Decent for Skinner's frustration but not memorable.

jokes.text: Principal Skinner, you're just stealing. / Welcome to Dick Cheney's America.
jokes.setup: Skinner taking equipment from private school
jokes.punchline: References political climate justifying theft
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire typical of Simpsons. Good topical reference with edge.

jokes.text: Mr. Puck, you make the only grub what satisfies my gut worm, I swear.
jokes.setup: Fancy chef Wolfgang Puck at elite event
jokes.punchline: Cletus's crude compliment mentioning parasites
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus characterization - trying to be complimentary but revealing disturbing personal details.

jokes.text: They are wasabi-infused with a portobello glaze. And you can buy them at the airport.
jokes.setup: Fancy chef describing gourmet Rice Krispie squares
jokes.punchline: Undermines exclusivity by mentioning airport availability
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of celebrity chef commercialization. Great structure deflating pretension.

jokes.text: I make mine with M&M's. / With M&M's? Now that's what I call fusion.
jokes.setup: Gourmet chef discussing sophisticated cuisine
jokes.punchline: Takes hillbilly's crude addition seriously as 'fusion' cuisine
jokes.explanation: Good satire of pretentious food culture accepting any combination as 'fusion.'

jokes.text: No one's doing any bumping. Time to ram-a-lam these ding-dongs.
jokes.setup: Bumper car ride at fair
jokes.punchline: Homer's aggressive nonsense language for bumper car strategy
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer made-up words. Fits character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: This is going right to the orphanage.
jokes.setup: Ned wins $100 at auction
jokes.punchline: Immediately donating winnings, making Homer's scheme backfire
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned characterization that frustrates Homer's plan. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Homer's auction sabotage backfiring: wins $100 bill for $50, Ned donates it to orphanage
jokes.setup: Homer trying to make Ned overpay at auction
jokes.punchline: Homer ends up losing money while Ned does good deed
jokes.explanation: Classic karma comedy - Homer's scheme backfires and highlights Ned's goodness

jokes.text: Look at her cry! New money always cries!
jokes.setup: Rich kids bullying girl about her backpack
jokes.punchline: Dismissive attitude toward 'new money' families
jokes.explanation: Sharp class satire about old money snobbery. Economical and pointed.

jokes.text: The name is Jackington.
jokes.setup: Bart tells bully to drop backpack, calling him Jack
jokes.punchline: Pretentious correction to formal name ending
jokes.explanation: Perfect rich kid pretension in one word. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: To the Hamptons!
jokes.setup: Rich kids retreating from fight
jokes.punchline: Fleeing to exclusive vacation destination
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist class comedy. Perfect rich kid retreat location.

jokes.text: Hey, butler, stop butling yourself.
jokes.setup: Butler being ordered around
jokes.punchline: Made-up verb form 'butling' for butler activities
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing rich kid entitlement but not exceptional.

jokes.text: McBain, Officer Nick Vengeance, Sergeant Murder, and I was a voice on the Frasier.
jokes.setup: Listing tough action movie roles
jokes.punchline: Ends with prestigious but gentle sitcom voice work
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection from violent roles to classy voice work. Great structure.

jokes.text: Come. We play Uno. I chase you with hose. No biggie.
jokes.setup: Inviting Bart to play at his house
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of bizarre violent games alongside normal ones
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy mixing innocent and threatening activities casually. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: In South Carolina. / Oh! I will not be a Gamecock!
jokes.setup: Homer promising Lisa can go to finest college
jokes.punchline: Lisa's horror at school mascot name
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's pride and the double meaning of mascot name.

jokes.text: Don't give them nicknames like 'Jumbo' or 'Boxcar,' and always get receipts. Makes you look like a business guy.
jokes.setup: Homer giving Bart advice about women
jokes.punchline: Terrible nickname advice paired with unrelated business tip
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer wisdom - completely wrong advice delivered with confidence. Great character voice.

jokes.text: One highway, zero city.
jokes.setup: Asked about car's mileage
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated poor fuel efficiency
jokes.explanation: Great economy and structure. Perfect absurdist take on gas mileage.

jokes.text: If you don't shut your big yap, I will rip off your face and use it as a napkin!
jokes.setup: Bart comments that Wolfcastle doesn't sound tough in person
jokes.punchline: Suddenly switches to extreme movie voice and threat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing actor's ability to switch personas. Solid structure.

jokes.text: Wow! The Q-tip he used to kill Ross Perot.
jokes.setup: Looking at movie props from shrinking movie
jokes.punchline: Mundane item used for absurd assassination
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy combining mundane object with political figure. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, it's okay. My bed is stuffed with hay. / (LAUGHING) Bart, you're so funny.
jokes.setup: Greta asks about Bart's house
jokes.punchline: Bart's honest poverty description mistaken for humor
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy about class differences and missed communication. Well-structured.

jokes.text: We shot this at 4:00 in the morning, and the crew was getting a little cranky. You can never get enough takes for Steven Soderbergh.
jokes.setup: DVD commentary over cartoon violence
jokes.punchline: Pretentious film commentary over silly cartoon
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke satirizing pretentious DVD commentaries on lowbrow content.

jokes.text: Homer, I see your daughter is one of those whale-kissing, Dukakis-hugging moon maidens.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking for vegetarian options
jokes.punchline: Wolfcastle's string of conservative stereotypes about liberals
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing action star's political views through creative insults.

jokes.text: He's my best friend because... Well, geographical convenience, really.
jokes.setup: Introducing Milhouse as best friend
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest reason for friendship
jokes.explanation: Exceptional line that's both funny and surprisingly honest about childhood friendships. Perfect economy and character voice.

jokes.text: I'm wearing my bathing suit under my pants.
jokes.setup: Meeting Greta for first time
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate oversharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse behavior - awkward oversharing. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Chuck. I live in his trunk.
jokes.setup: Wolfcastle calls for his look-alike
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of living in car trunk
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment with perfect deadpan delivery. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Oh, that's just Lara Flynn Boyle. / I have a Boyle on my ass!
jokes.setup: Homer sits on something sharp at basketball game
jokes.punchline: Pun on actress's name and medical condition
jokes.explanation: Clever celebrity name pun with good setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: I just hope the audience is kind because my material is weak, and I have that bladder thing.
jokes.setup: Skinner preparing for stand-up comedy
jokes.punchline: Pre-emptive admission of failure plus TMI
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner characterization - anxious and oversharing. Good cringe setup.

jokes.text: This is the biggest thing that's happened to me since chocolate milk! / They've got chocolate milk now?
jokes.setup: Bart excited about Skinner's comedy show
jokes.punchline: Homer focuses on wrong part of statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - missing the point completely. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Have you ever noticed how the dumb kids fill in the wrong circles?
jokes.setup: Skinner doing stand-up about standardized testing
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate joke about students from their principal
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad comedy showing Skinner's poor judgment. Works for character but not funny itself.

jokes.text: I think I heard California surfer.
jokes.setup: Audience shouts 'Loser Principal' for improv
jokes.punchline: Skinner mishears or deliberately ignores insult
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Skinner's desperate denial. Character-driven humor.

jokes.text: Lisa, I only lied because it was the easiest way to get what I wanted.
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizing Bart for lying to Greta
jokes.punchline: Bart's brutally honest admission about deception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - completely honest about being dishonest. Great character voice.

jokes.text: Lise, women are easy. State capitals are hard!
jokes.setup: Lisa sarcastically says Bart understands women
jokes.punchline: Bart's completely wrong assessment of difficulty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant child logic completely missing Lisa's sarcasm. Perfect economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Cockfight.
jokes.setup: Greta asks where he was
jokes.punchline: Casual one-word response about illegal activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character moment. Maximum funny per word ratio.

jokes.text: Lou, you can't leave the force! I can change! / I just think there's more money in private security. / All I'm hearing is I'm too fat.
jokes.setup: Background conversation at breakup location
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's insecurity misinterpreting professional decision
jokes.explanation: Good background gag showing Wiggum's insecurity. Solid character work.

jokes.text: No. Let my muscles hug you.
jokes.setup: Greta crying about breakup
jokes.punchline: Action star's unusual comfort method
jokes.explanation: Perfect action star parenting approach. Good character consistency and quotability.

jokes.text: Except he kissed me in the eye.
jokes.setup: Showing drawing of kiss with Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Revealing Milhouse's poor aim
jokes.explanation: Good characterization of Milhouse's inexperience. Solid cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Canada? Why should we leave America to visit America Junior?
jokes.setup: Bart wants to go to Toronto
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive view of Canada
jokes.explanation: Classic American stereotypical view of Canada. Good character moment.

jokes.text: It's so clean and bland. I'm home!
jokes.setup: Family arrives in Canada
jokes.punchline: Ned's appreciation for Canadian blandness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned characterization appreciating Canada's wholesome reputation. Good stereotype humor.

jokes.text: American currency! What time would you like your breakfast, sir?
jokes.setup: Bart offers US dollar to Canadian
jokes.punchline: Extreme servility toward American money
jokes.explanation: Decent stereotype humor about currency exchange and Canadian deference to Americans.

jokes.text: Oh, I see you drive on the left up here. / No, Ma'am. I'm drunk.
jokes.setup: Observing unusual driving pattern
jokes.punchline: Driver's honest admission of intoxication
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect structure. Dark humor delivered with great economy.

jokes.text: The geek shall inherit the earth.
jokes.setup: Nerdy character being bullied
jokes.punchline: Biblical reference adapted for nerd empowerment
jokes.explanation: Decent biblical reference parody but fairly predictable for nerd character.

jokes.text: My therapist said that's all I could ever hope for.
jokes.setup: Bart says Greta only dates Milhouse to get back at him
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's acceptance of being rebound relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse characterization - low self-esteem validated by therapy. Dark but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Both our viewers must be thrilled.
jokes.setup: Commentating on curling match
jokes.punchline: Self-aware joke about sport's low viewership
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor about Canadian sports broadcasting. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: I told you! I don't know how that scrunchie got in my hair!
jokes.setup: Greta says she wants someone more masculine
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's defensive response about feminine hair accessory
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Milhouse's lack of masculinity and denial. Fits character perfectly.

jokes.text: I can't believe I have to give her half my stuff!
jokes.setup: Being dumped after short relationship
jokes.punchline: Acting like it's a divorce with property division
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor applying adult relationship rules to children. Character-appropriate overreaction.

jokes.text: I can't believe we're on the Canadian Olympic Basketball team. / Yep. It's just that easy.
jokes.setup: Boys in Canada looking for fun
jokes.punchline: Immediately joining Olympic team with no qualification
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist ending showing Canada's supposed lower athletic standards. Solid closing gag.

jokes.text: How come you always run out of tardy slips before you run out of permission slips? / How come you suck?
jokes.setup: Student asking logical administrative question
jokes.punchline: Kearney's dismissive non-answer
jokes.explanation: Basic bully response to reasonable question. Works for character but not particularly clever.

tmdb_overview: Bart befriends Rainer Wolfcastle's daughter Greta. She has a crush on Bart, but he does not seem to realize it and eventually stops seeing her. Seeking revenge, Greta begins dating Bart's best friend Milhouse, which causes Bart to start missing her. She leaves for Toronto with her father, and Bart convinces his family to follow them there.
